---
title: "scRNA Annotation Method Report: scDeepSort"
date: today
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: 3
    toc-location: left
    html-math-method: katex
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
    html-table-processing: none
    theme: lumen
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 5
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

from IPython.display import Markdown, display
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

LIB_DIR = Path(".").resolve() / "lib"
if str(LIB_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(LIB_DIR))

from report_utils import format_table, load_report_context, package_versions_table, style_bar

ROOT = Path(".").resolve()
context = load_report_context(ROOT)
```

## Current Status

```{python}
display(
    Markdown(
        """
This is a **scaffold report** for a future Python-native **scDeepSort**
backend. It is kept here so the multi-report structure is stable even before
this backend is operational.
"""
    )
)
```

```{python}
status_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"field": "method", "value": "scdeepsort"},
        {"field": "implemented_in_runtime", "value": "no"},
        {"field": "intended_role", "value": "pretrained deep-learning annotation in supported species/settings"},
        {"field": "selected_in_current_run", "value": "scdeepsort" in context["selected_methods"]},
    ]
)
format_table(status_df)
```

## Why This Backend Is Conditional

```{python}
fit_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"consideration": "species support", "impact": "only suitable for supported organisms and pretrained settings"},
        {"consideration": "runtime compatibility", "impact": "older dependency constraints need careful handling"},
        {"consideration": "model scope", "impact": "tissue/model mismatches can reduce annotation quality quickly"},
    ]
)
format_table(fit_df)
```

```{python}
profile_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"dimension": "pretrained convenience", "score": 4},
        {"dimension": "environment stability", "score": 2},
        {"dimension": "species/model specificity", "score": 4},
        {"dimension": "general-purpose flexibility", "score": 2},
        {"dimension": "benchmark interest", "score": 4},
    ]
)
profile_plot = px.bar(
    profile_df,
    x="dimension",
    y="score",
    title="Expected scDeepSort profile in this template",
    range_y=[0, 5.5],
)
style_bar(profile_plot, height=420)
```

## Paper And Documentation Takeaways

The scDeepSort paper presents a pretrained graph-based deep-learning annotation
approach benchmarked against multiple annotation tools on external human and
mouse datasets. The current documentation also makes the environment constraints
very explicit, which is a major practical consideration for whether this
backend belongs in a production template.

```{python}
takeaways_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"takeaway": "pretrained annotation focus", "implication": "attractive when the supported model scope matches the dataset well"},
        {"takeaway": "benchmark-driven positioning", "implication": "worth testing comparatively rather than trusting by default"},
        {"takeaway": "narrower runtime compatibility", "implication": "dependency handling is a first-class design issue here"},
        {"takeaway": "species and model coverage matter", "implication": "mismatch risk should be visible in the report"},
    ]
)
format_table(takeaways_df)
```

## When scDeepSort Would Be Worth Testing

```{python}
fit_cases_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"situation": "human or mouse dataset with a supported pretrained model", "recommendation": "reasonable comparison backend"},
        {"situation": "strong need for quick pretrained annotation", "recommendation": "potentially useful if the environment is stable"},
        {"situation": "unsupported species or tissue context", "recommendation": "poor fit"},
        {"situation": "strict production reproducibility requirements", "recommendation": "adopt cautiously until dependency handling is mature"},
    ]
)
format_table(fit_cases_df)
```

## Required Future Inputs

```{python}
planned_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"input": "species", "purpose": "select the supported organism"},
        {"input": "pretrained tissue/model", "purpose": "pick a compatible model family"},
        {"input": "gene symbol compatibility", "purpose": "align the query matrix to model expectations"},
        {"input": "runtime environment summary", "purpose": "document compatibility-sensitive runs"},
    ]
)
format_table(planned_df)
```

## Planned Report Contents

```{python}
diagnostics_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"planned_section": "predicted label distribution", "content": "check for implausible class imbalance"},
        {"planned_section": "cluster agreement", "content": "compare scDeepSort labels to unsupervised clusters"},
        {"planned_section": "uncertainty summary", "content": "flag cells that should not be auto-accepted"},
        {"planned_section": "environment summary", "content": "track version-sensitive execution details"},
    ]
)
format_table(diagnostics_df)
```

## Operational Risks To Surface In The Report

```{python}
risk_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"risk": "model mismatch", "why_it_matters": "pretrained labels can look confident but still be biologically off-target"},
        {"risk": "dependency fragility", "why_it_matters": "older runtime constraints can make reproducibility harder than with lighter backends"},
        {"risk": "narrow support scope", "why_it_matters": "coverage limitations should remain visible to end users"},
    ]
)
format_table(risk_df)
```

## Readiness Checklist

```{python}
checklist_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"task": "pin a maintainable dependency set", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "add species/model config handling", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "write scDeepSort output tables", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "add method-specific review figures", "status": "pending"},
    ]
)
format_table(checklist_df)
```

```{python}
checklist_plot = px.bar(
    checklist_df.assign(done=[0, 0, 0, 0]),
    x="task",
    y="done",
    title="scDeepSort implementation status",
)
style_bar(checklist_plot, height=420)
```

## References Consulted

```{python}
references_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "resource": "scDeepSort paper",
            "role": "benchmark framing and intended method scope",
            "link": "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34500471/",
        },
        {
            "resource": "scDeepSort documentation",
            "role": "runtime and installation constraints",
            "link": "https://scdeepsort.readthedocs.io/en/master/get_started.html",
        },
    ]
)
format_table(references_df)
```

## Session Info

```{python}
session_df = package_versions_table(["scanpy", "anndata", "pandas", "numpy", "plotly", "scdeepsort"])
format_table(session_df)
```
